ksa_text: Skill in triaging data pipeline incidents, including failure identification, impact assessment, escalation decision-making, status communication to affected consumers, and remediation coordination.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Operations & Program Management KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.443) to justify inclusion for the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. Without stronger framework alignment, this skill KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain skill KSA be expected in real-world Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Operations & Program Management is not a standard competency expectation for Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.443) and edge-domain placement (Operations & Program Management) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. All passes support exclusion.
